← All worlds/World 04
World 04 · 1 state

Social Anxiety

Films for everyone whose nervous system reads connection as danger — and the slow, stubborn act of staying in the room anyway.

Choose an emotion
Fear of Connection
On Fear of Connection

You want to be known. You also know, with bone-deep certainty, that if someone got close enough to actually know you, they would leave. So you build just enough warmth to seem approachable, and just enough distance to feel safe. And then you go home and feel the paradox of it. These films are for the nervous system that reads intimacy as danger — and for the stubborn, irrational part of you that keeps trying anyway. Not because the fear goes away. But because something keeps being worth the risk.

Fear of Connection

Films for the part of you that wants to be known and can’t bear it.

CS8.6
2012

The Perks of Being a Wallflower

Stephen Chbosky

View Film →

A shy, brilliant freshman with a complicated past is taken in by two senior misfits and slowly learns what it means to be chosen.

CS9.2
2013

Her

Spike Jonze

View Film →

A lonely letter-writer in near-future Los Angeles falls in love with an AI operating system designed to learn him. A slow, devastating arc of being known.

CS8.9
2018

Eighth Grade

Bo Burnham

View Film →

A thirteen-year-old YouTuber tries to survive the last week of middle school, armed with too much advice and not enough audience.

CS8.7
2002

Punch-Drunk Love

Paul Thomas Anderson

View Film →

A small-business owner with rage issues, seven sisters, and a phone-sex scam after him, falls for a woman who isn’t scared of any of it.

CS8.3
2007

Lars and the Real Girl

Craig Gillespie

View Film →

A painfully shy young man introduces a life-sized doll to his small town as his girlfriend — and the town, to its credit, plays along.

CS8.9
2001

Amelie

Jean-Pierre Jeunet

View Film →

A shy waitress in Montmartre decides to make small, secret improvements to the lives of strangers — until one of them turns into a love story she has to step into.

CS9.0
2016

A Silent Voice

Naoko Yamada

View Film →

A young man who bullied a deaf classmate in elementary school tries, years later, to apologise — and the film follows what repair actually looks like.

CS8.1
2022

Cha Cha Real Smooth

Cooper Raiff

View Film →

A directionless college graduate becomes a bar-mitzvah party-starter and forms a complicated, careful friendship with a young mother and her autistic daughter.

CS8.7
2017

Columbus

Kogonada

View Film →

A young woman caring for her mother in Columbus, Indiana, befriends a Korean-American man waiting on his dying father — and they talk, slowly, about everything.

CS8.4
2010

Submarine

Richard Ayoade

View Film →

A self-narrating Welsh teenager tries to lose his virginity, save his parents’ marriage, and play the leading role in his own life — all at once.

CS8.4
2015

Krisha

Trey Edward Shults

View Film →

A woman returns to her family for Thanksgiving after years of estrangement. The film follows her through the day as her sobriety begins to crack.

CS7.9
2013

Palo Alto

Gia Coppola

View Film →

Suburban California teenagers drift through parties, soccer, and bad decisions. Based on James Franco’s short stories.

CS7.9
2014

Welcome to Me

Shira Piven

View Film →

A woman with borderline personality disorder wins the lottery and spends it producing her own talk show about herself.

CS8.0
2019

The Art of Self-Defense

Riley Stearns

View Film →

A meek accountant mugged on the street joins a karate dojo. The more he learns, the more he is remade into something he does not recognise.

CS8.0
2015

Me and Earl and the Dying Girl

Alfonso Gomez-Rejon

View Film →

A socially avoidant high school senior is forced by his mother to befriend a classmate diagnosed with leukaemia. He makes films.

CS8.1
2018

Love, Simon

Greg Berlanti

View Film →

A sixteen-year-old at an Atlanta high school navigates coming out via an anonymous email exchange while being blackmailed by a classmate.

CS7.8
2018

Beautiful Boy

Felix Van Groeningen

View Film →

A journalist father and his teenage son navigate the son’s methamphetamine addiction across several years and relapses.

CS8.5
2014

The Way He Looks

Daniel Ribeiro

View Film →

A blind teenager in São Paulo navigates first love with a new classmate while managing his best friend’s jealousy.

CS8.1
2010

Please Give

Nicole Holofcener

View Film →

A New York couple who own a vintage furniture store await the death of the neighbour whose apartment they want to buy. Everyone is made uncomfortable by this.

CS8.0
2018

Green Book

Peter Farrelly

View Film →

A white bouncer drives a Black classical pianist through the Jim Crow South in 1962. An unlikely friendship forms.

CS8.1
2022

Cha Cha Real Smooth

Cooper Raiff

View Film →

A directionless college graduate becomes a bar-mitzvah party-starter and forms a careful friendship with a young mother and her autistic daughter.

CS8.6
2012

The Perks of Being a Wallflower

Stephen Chbosky

View Film →

A shy freshman is taken in by two senior misfits. The friendship begins to unlock something that has been locked for a long time.

CS8.6
2015

Anomalisa

Charlie Kaufman & Duke Johnson

View Film →

A customer-service motivational speaker in Cincinnati is consumed by dread that every person he meets is the same. Until he meets one who is different.

CS8.1
2018

A Quiet Place

John Krasinski

View Film →

A family on a post-apocalyptic farm must live in near-total silence to survive creatures that hunt by sound.

Next world · 05
Self-Worth →

For when you keep checking the bar nobody told you you were measuring against.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
